englishRequirements.waiverNotes: Non-native speakers of English should submit TOEFL, IELTS, MET, or similar scores. U-M notes that students who do not wish to have SAT or ACT scores considered can still apply without them.

scholarshipNotes: U-M says it is committed to need-based affordability for Michigan residents, offers scholarships and grants through financial aid, and some scholarships are merit-based. International students on temporary visas are not eligible for financial aid and are expected to pay the full cost of attendance.

testingRequirements.latestSatTestDateNote: Self-reported SAT or ACT scores for Early Decision/Early Action are optional and due by Nov. 1; for Regular Decision they are optional and due by Feb. 1.
testingRequirements.latestActTestDateNote: Self-reported SAT or ACT scores for Early Decision/Early Action are optional and due by Nov. 1; for Regular Decision they are optional and due by Feb. 1.

identity.schoolName.excerpt: University of Michigan Office of Undergraduate Admissions

applicationRounds.excerpt: The University of Michigan offers three application plans for first-year applicants: Early Decision, Early Action, and Regular Decision.

deadlinesByRound.excerpt: Nov 1 ... Early Decision for First-year Students Only ... Early Action for First-year Students Only ... Feb 1 ... Fall Term Application Deadline

englishRequirements.excerpt: TOEFL, IELTS, MET, etc. scores (for non-native speakers of English)

testPolicy.excerpt: SAT or ACT score, if available and you wish them to be considered
students who do not wish to have SAT or ACT scores considered ... can still submit other optional forms of testing

requiredMaterials.excerpt: Completed Common Application with $75 application fee ... High school transcript ... School Report ... One Teacher Evaluation
University of Michigan Questions ... Common Application Personal Essay

scholarshipAvailabilityFlag.excerpt: Michigan has numerous resources and ways to help eligible students and their families pay for college.

scholarshipNotes.excerpt: U-M is the only public university in Michigan that covers 100 percent of demonstrated financial need of eligible Michigan residents.
financial aid may contain ... institutional scholarships and grants
There is no federal financial aid and limited scholarship funding available to international students attending U-M

research is central to the university's mission

warnings: The admissions checklist allows test scores to be self-reported and only if the applicant wants them considered, so testingRequirements.scoreReportingPolicy is recorded as self_report_allowed.

The cost page publishes both resident and nonresident budgets; tuitionAnnualUsd and estimatedCostOfAttendanceUsd use the published nonresident lower-division figures for a single statewide sticker price.
